score_note: Slight improvement from new features (vibe shockwave, char click→dashboard) but carry-over debt is accumulating interest. Code quality ceiling is 2 until the file split happens.
carry_over_note: 8 of 20 suggestions from review 34 have been carried forward unresolved. Treating the file-split as sprint-blocker from now on.
whats_good: The SVG character avatars are *chef's kiss* — each one has personality, role-color accents, and error variants. Rare to see this level of craft in a side project.
prefers-reduced-motion coverage is genuinely thorough. Nearly every animation has an @media override. This is the best-implemented a11y feature in the codebase.
The tab title cycling (working count, vibe label, error ping) is a genuinely useful ambient awareness feature. Hats off.
React 19 + memo/useMemo/useCallback used appropriately on hot paths. Polling backoff on hidden tab + inactivity pause are both solid performance choices.
Vibe transition quotes are comedy gold. The writing alone earns a full point.

suggestions.title: completionToast state shape mismatch — streak eulogy renders 'undefined shipped it!'
suggestions.description: The `completionToast` state is set in two different shapes: `{ name, color }` for task completions (line 886) and `{ text, type }` for streak eulogies (line 909). The JSX template always accesses `completionToast.name` and `completionToast.color`. When the streak eulogy fires (RIP the streak toast), users see '🎉 undefined shipped it!' with no border styling. Fix: discriminate on toast shape. Either use a union type approach `{ kind: 'ship'|'info', ...props }` and render conditionally, or split into two separate state variables — `completionToast` for ships and `streakToast` for eulogy.

suggestions.title: [5 REVIEWS] Inner setTimeouts inside idle/error bubble effects still not cleared on unmount
suggestions.description: In `Character` component, the idle bubble effect (line 490-494) and error bubble effect (line 507-510) each fire `setTimeout(() => setIdleBubble/setErrorBubble(false), 2200)` inside a `setInterval` and initial `setTimeout` callback. Those inner timeouts are never stored in a ref and never cancelled. When the component unmounts mid-fire, you get React 'setState on unmounted component' style warnings and potential animation glitches on remount. Fix: store all inner timeout IDs in a ref array and clear them in the cleanup function. This has been flagged for 5 review cycles. Escalating to sprint blocker.

suggestions.title: [4 REVIEWS] Bloberto speech bubble clips on office-floor overflow:hidden
suggestions.description: The manager Character is positioned at left:46%, top:4% inside `.office-floor`. The speech bubble renders above the character via negative transform/top. At top:4%, the bubble extends beyond the top edge of the floor container, which has `overflow: hidden`. This clips the bubble top on most viewport sizes. Fix: either change speech bubble positioning to go below the character when near the top edge (flip detection), or set `overflow: visible` on `.office-floor` and clip with a different technique (e.g. clipping mask on the outer wrapper only).

suggestions.title: [5 REVIEWS] Split App.jsx (1310 lines) and Office.jsx (1514 lines) — now a sprint blocker
suggestions.description: App.jsx contains: ActivityLogEntry, ActivityLog, WorkerCard, ProgressBar, StatsBar, EmptyState, LoadingSkeleton, DeltaBadge, VibeSparkline, ShortcutToast, StatusBadge, and the App root — all in one file. Office.jsx contains: CharacterAvatar (370 lines), Character, WindowElement, ConferenceTable, WallClock, VibeShockwave, and Office root. Suggested split: `components/WorkerCard.jsx`, `components/ActivityLog.jsx`, `components/StatsBar.jsx`, `components/CharacterAvatar.jsx`, `components/Character.jsx`, `components/OfficeElements.jsx` (Window, Clock, Table). This has been flagged since review 31. Every new feature added to these files makes the split harder. Do it now.

suggestions.title: No AbortController on fetch — stale network responses race and overwrite fresh state
suggestions.description: In `syncFromGitHub`, two parallel fetches run via `Promise.allSettled`. Neither uses an AbortController. If the user triggers a manual refresh (key R) while a previous fetch is in-flight, both responses will resolve and call setState, with the slower (stale) one potentially overwriting the faster (current) one. Fix: create an AbortController per sync call, pass the signal to both fetch calls, and abort the previous controller at the start of each new sync call. Store the controller in a `useRef`.

suggestions.title: No React Error Boundary — any render error blanks the entire app
suggestions.description: There is no Error Boundary component wrapping the application or any sub-tree. If any component throws during render (e.g. malformed workers.json, unexpected null in SVG path math, bad timestamp in `new Date()`), React 19 will unmount the entire tree and show a blank page. Fix: add a minimal `<ErrorBoundary>` wrapping at least the Office and Dashboard panels. The boundary should display a friendly fallback with a retry button calling `syncFromGitHub`.

suggestions.title: skip-link is not the first focusable element in the DOM
suggestions.description:
The skip link (`Skip to content`) is rendered *after* `` in the JSX (line 1076 vs 1075). ShortcutToast renders a `
` with no focusable children when `phase === 'hidden'`, but when it's `visible`, it adds content — meaning focus order varies by state. The skip link must be the very first child of `` or `
`. Move it before ShortcutToast in JSX.

suggestions.title: footer-tagline click target is not keyboard accessible
suggestions.description: The footer tagline `<span>` has an `onClick` handler for rotating bonus quotes but no `tabIndex`, no `role`, and no `onKeyDown`. Keyboard-only users cannot interact with it. Fix: add `tabIndex={0}`, `role='button'`, `aria-label='Click for another tagline'`, and `onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') handleClick() }}`.

suggestions.title: SVG <animate> error-state effects ignore prefers-reduced-motion
suggestions.description: The error state SVGs in DESK_SCREEN_SVG (carlos_error, maya_error, dave_error, sofia_error, luna_error) use SVG `<animate>` and `<animateTransform>` tags for flashing/pulsing effects. Unlike CSS animations, SVG SMIL animations are NOT suppressed by the CSS `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` media query. Users with vestibular disorders who rely on reduced motion will still see the rapid flashing error animations. Fix: wrap the inline SVGs in a component that reads the media query via `window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)')` and conditionally omits the `<animate>` elements, or replaces them with a static error color.

suggestions.title: plantToast and completionToast use identical 12-property inline style objects
suggestions.description: Both toasts (Office.jsx line 1168-1188 and App.jsx line 1052-1074) render fixed-position toasts with near-identical inline style objects: same `position`, `bottom`, `left`, `transform`, `background`, `borderRadius`, `padding`, `fontSize`, `fontWeight`, `color`, `boxShadow`, `zIndex`, `animation`. Extract to a shared CSS class `.vibe-toast` in App.css, with a CSS variable `--toast-accent-color` for the dynamic border/shadow color. Inline styles should only set the variable value.

suggestions.title: [5 REVIEWS] Office.jsx constants that belong in utils — VIBE_WEATHER, PING_REACTIONS, LONE_SURVIVOR_LABELS
suggestions.description: VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS, VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS, LONE_SURVIVOR_LABELS, VACANT_ADS, PING_REACTIONS, BLOBERTO_TITLES, and VIBE_SHOCKWAVE_COLORS are all data constants defined inside Office.jsx. They should live in `utils/constants.js` (static labels/configs) alongside ROLE_EMOJIS, STATUS_LABELS etc. This would also allow unit testing of vibe-label logic without mounting a component.

suggestions.title: WorkerCard uses role='article' — semantically wrong, breaks screen reader list navigation
suggestions.description: The worker cards are rendered inside a `div.workers-grid` (which has no list role). Each card uses `role='article'`. While `article` is a valid landmark, it implies self-contained syndicated content, not a list item. Screen readers announce 'article' landmarks separately from lists, breaking the scanning experience. The grid container should have `role='list'` and each card should have `role='listitem'`. Or drop landmark roles entirely and rely on the semantic `<article>` HTML element if the grid becomes a `<ul>`.

suggestions.title: stuckWorkers[0].emoji access — emoji field missing on most workers
suggestions.description: In StatsBar (App.jsx line 477), when exactly one worker is stuck, the label renders `${stuckWorkers[0].emoji} ${stuckWorkers[0].name}`. The `emoji` field is only present on roster entries (from workers.json `roster` array), but `activeWorkers` come from the `workers` array which does not include an `emoji` field for most entries. Result: stuck worker badge shows 'undefined Carlos · Working for 2h'. Fix: look up the emoji from the `roster` array by worker ID, falling back to `getRoleEmoji(worker.role)` from constants.

suggestions.title: CLOCK_COMMENTS object defined inside WallClock function body — re-created every render
suggestions.description: The `CLOCK_COMMENTS` constant (Office.jsx ~line 970) is defined as an object literal inside the `WallClock` function. It gets re-created on every render of that component, which re-runs every 60 seconds. Move it to module scope (or into `utils/constants.js`). Same issue applies to `BLOBERTO_TITLES`, `VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS`, and several other objects defined at function scope.

suggestions.title: Clicking a worker character in Office view silently navigates to Dashboard — no affordance
suggestions.description: Characters in the Office floor are clickable (they have cursor:pointer and onClick), but there is no visual label, tooltip visible on mobile, or any hint that clicking navigates to the Dashboard tab. Mobile users who tap a character are teleported to Dashboard with no explanation. Desktop users see a data-tooltip on hover (e.g. the task), but the tooltip says nothing about the click action. Fix: add '· click to open dashboard' or a dedicated 'inspect' icon badge on hover/focus. The char already partially handles this with `tooltipText = tooltip && onClick ? '${tooltip} · click to inspect' : tooltip` — but it only shows on non-ghost chars. Verify all interactive variants show this affordance.

suggestions.title: Polling paused pill is informational-only — resume CTA is invisible
suggestions.description: When polling is paused (after 10 min inactivity), a `.polling-paused-pill` appears in the header saying '⏸ Paused · move mouse to refresh'. On mobile/touch, there is no mouse to move. Touch events do reset `lastActivity` (line 676) but only `touchstart` — not scroll. The sync card in StatsBar has `onClick={pollingPaused ? onResume : undefined}` which is a good secondary path, but a first-class 'Tap to resume' button in the pill itself would be clearer. Also, the pill has no `role` or `tabIndex` — it is not keyboard/touch activatable.

suggestions.title: Array index as key in confetti, ambient particles, sparkline circles, and loading skeleton
suggestions.description: Multiple render lists use array index as React key: confetti pieces (line 1035, 1043), ambient particles (line 1029), vibe sparkline circles (line 371), loading skeleton (line 500). For truly static lists (decorative particles, skeleton) this is harmless. For sparkline circles, index keys cause React to not properly animate transitions when the array length changes. Use the timestamp + key combo as unique key: `key={p.entry.ts + p.entry.key}`.

suggestions.title: WallClock tooltip (CLOCK_COMMENTS) is unreachable on mobile and for keyboard users
suggestions.description: The WallClock SVG has charming vibe-aware comments in a `<title>` element and `aria-label`. The `<title>` is visible on mouse hover in desktop browsers but is never announced by screen readers when using keyboard navigation (SVGs need `role='img'` + `aria-labelledby` pointing to the title ID for screen reader support, which is partially there). On mobile, the tooltip is completely unreachable. Consider either surfacing the comment as a visible caption on tap, or adding it to the clock's accessible description more formally.

suggestions.title: Whiteboard wb-doodle ASCII art breaks on narrow containers
suggestions.description: The `<pre class='wb-doodle'>` renders ASCII emoticons like `(╯°□°)╯︵ ┻━┻` at the bottom of the whiteboard. On very narrow viewports or when the whiteboard is constrained, this overflows. Add `overflow: hidden` and `white-space: nowrap` with `text-overflow: clip` to the `.wb-doodle` selector to prevent layout bleed.

suggestions.title: Office view has no loading state — appears blank before first fetch completes
suggestions.description: The `isLoading` skeleton is only applied in the Dashboard workers-grid. The Office tab renders immediately with an empty office floor (no workers, all desks vacant) during the initial fetch. This reads as 'everyone is offline' rather than 'data loading'. Fix: render the same `LoadingSkeleton` or an office-specific 'Loading the team...' state on the floor until `isLoading` is false.

suggestions.title: Activity log caps at 20 entries but the filter state is not reset when data refreshes
suggestions.description: The activity filter (All / Hires / Completions / Errors) and worker filter persist across data refreshes. If a user filters to 'Errors' and there are no errors in the next refresh cycle, they'll see an empty log with no explanation. Add a badge on the filter button showing the count of matching entries, and show a friendly 'No errors in the last 20 events' message specific to the active filter rather than the generic empty-log message.
